_meta.keyKnowledge: We learn about different worldviews we can see in familiar places, like festivals, sacred sites and caring for places and living things.

_source.w: We learn about different worldviews we can see in familiar places, like festivals, sacred sites and caring for places and living things.
y: Worldview diversity is all around us — at festivals, in nature and in how people care for the world.
Understanding different worldviews helps us appreciate why people relate to places and living things differently.
Learning about worldviews builds our respect for different ways of understanding the world.
eg: We visit or learn about a local festival that reflects a particular worldview.
We discuss how different cultures care for the environment in different ways.
We learn about sacred sites and why they are important to certain communities.
We explore how Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples care for Country as part of their worldview.
We discuss how different religions or belief systems celebrate nature and living things.
We share how our own family shows care for places and other living things.
